customPrompts.text: What did I almost talk myself out of this week, and did I do it anyway?
Whose voice was I hearing in my head this week?

goals.note: Don't grind past 8 — leave room for life.
8s are great. 9s mean I'm overdoing it.

goodThings.text: Coffee on the porch on Saturday morning
goodThings.why: Because I went to bed early Friday. Compounding effect.

goodThings.text: Finished the cabinet I'd been avoiding
goodThings.why: Because I gave myself 25 minutes instead of telling myself it had to be the whole afternoon.
Because I asked the question I'd been avoiding instead of the small-talk one.
Because I took meetings on foot.

goodThings.text: Read for an hour without picking up my phone
goodThings.why: Because I left it in the other room.

goodThings.text: Cooked the meal I'd been thinking about
goodThings.why: Because Saturday had no other commitments.

promptResponses.response: Reached out to Sam — we hadn't talked in months. Took 90 seconds.
The dread about Wednesday's meeting. I've been pretending it wasn't there.
Saturday morning. Working on the cabinet. Three hours felt like twenty minutes.
Last Sunday. Slept ten hours and woke up without an alarm. Felt almost foreign.
What I actually want my work to look like in five years vs what it looks like now.
When I open my laptop, the first thing I will do is open the doc I'm avoiding — not Slack.

Five years from now I live somewhere with a real morning. Coffee, the long walk, then work that I chose. The work is harder than now but I'm steadier inside it. There's a person I love and we know each other well by then. My body still works — I take it on long walks and it surprises me. I have time to make things badly. I read more than I scroll. Money isn't the constant background hum it is now. I'm closer to my parents than I ever expected to be.

To J:

I don't think I ever properly thanked you for the call last September. I was unraveling and didn't quite know it, and you noticed before I did. You asked one specific question that I'd been avoiding for months, and you didn't let me deflect. The answer took me three more weeks to find, but the question was the unlock. I don't think you've thought about that call since. I think about it almost weekly.
Self-compassion break · "The meeting felt like a verdict and I'd been carrying it around for three days."

About: Mom

RECEIVED
Time, mostly. So much of it. The phone calls every Sunday. The visits. The small noticing.

GIVEN
Less than I should have. I called less than she'd have liked. Showed up when I needed to, not when she did.

TROUBLES CAUSED
Long stretches of quiet she had to be patient through. The worry I gave her when I went silent. The conversations she wanted to have that I deferred.

reflections.prompt: Deep Work rose by 2 this week. What changed?
reflections.response: I closed Slack between 9 and 11 every morning. That's it. That was the whole trick.

reflections.prompt: Vitality dropped by 3. Anything worth naming?
reflections.response: Two nights of poor sleep + the meeting I'd been dreading. Body knew before I did.

reflections.prompt: Connection has been quiet — how is it?
reflections.response: I noticed I'd been letting texts pile up. Wrote back to three people today.

reflections.prompt: Inner World is at 4 this week. What would a 6 look like?
reflections.response: Less time on my phone before bed. Reading instead. The thing I keep saying I'll do.

reflections.prompt: What did this week ask of you that you weren't expecting?
reflections.response: Saying no to something I'd already agreed to. It was harder than I thought it would be.

reflections.prompt: What's a small thing from this week worth remembering?
reflections.response: Sunday morning walk with no phone. Heard birds I'd been missing.
